faq_items.question: Do I need an account to register for the reunion?
faq_items.answer: Yes. Creating a free account lets you register for events, update your profile, manage tickets, and save your schedule. You can create an account using the email address you used in the RSVP or any current email.

faq_items.question: What is the deadline to register for the reunion weekend?
faq_items.answer: We recommend registering by May 20, 2026 so we can provide accurate counts to the venue. Online registration will remain open until June 10, 2026, or until tickets sell out.

faq_items.question: What ticket types are available and what do they include?
faq_items.answer: We offer full-weekend passes, Friday-only, Saturday Evening Only, and Sunday-only options, plus discounted guest tickets. Each ticket description lists which meals and events are included so you can choose what fits your plans and budget.

faq_items.question: Can I change my ticket type after I register?
faq_items.answer: Yes, in most cases you can change your ticket type until June 5, 2026. Visit the Register / RSVP page, open your existing registration, and use the edit option. If you need help, contact the organizers using the Contact & Support form.

faq_items.question: How many guests can I bring to the reunion events?
faq_items.answer: For the Saturday Evening Gala you may bring up to two guests, subject to ticket availability. Other events may have different guest limits, which are listed in each event description and on the ticket selection screen.

faq_items.question: Can my guest choose a different meal preference than me?
faq_items.answer: Yes. During registration you will see a separate meal preference dropdown for each guest. You can select vegetarian, vegan, or other options independently for yourself and each guest.

faq_items.question: What payment methods do you accept for tickets and donations?
faq_items.answer: You can pay online using major credit or debit cards, or choose the Pay at Event option to settle your balance at check-in. For class gift donations, you may also pledge now and pay later by check or through the school’s giving portal.

faq_items.question: Who can see my profile information and bio?
faq_items.answer: You control your visibility. On the My Profile page you can choose to share details with classmates only, with all logged-in users, or keep certain fields private. Your email address is never displayed publicly.

faq_items.question: How do favorites work in the Classmate Directory?
faq_items.answer: In the directory, click the heart icon on a classmate card to add them to your favorites. You can then use the Favorites filter to quickly see people you’ve starred when viewing the seating chart or planning meetups.

faq_items.question: Can I volunteer for more than one shift during the weekend?
faq_items.answer: Yes, you can sign up for multiple shifts as long as the times do not overlap. The Volunteer page shows shift times and durations, and the summary panel calculates your total volunteer hours before you confirm.

faq_items.question: How do I choose my table for the Saturday dinner?
faq_items.answer: Visit the Seating & Tables page to view the interactive seating chart. You can filter to show tables with your favorited classmates and select available seats for yourself and your guests, then click Reserve Seats to save your choices.

faq_items.question: Are there any guidelines for posting on the Discussion Board?
faq_items.answer: Yes. Be respectful, avoid offensive or discriminatory language, and keep posts related to the class and reunion. Read the Code of Conduct for full guidelines. Moderators may remove content that violates these standards.

faq_items.question: Do you offer recommended hotels or group rates?
faq_items.answer: Yes. The Travel & Lodging page lists recommended hotels near the venue, including options with free breakfast and group rates. You can filter by price, distance, and amenities, then save your choice to the My Trip page.

faq_items.question: Who can I contact about accessibility needs or special accommodations?
faq_items.answer: Please use the Contact & Support form or email the organizers directly. We will work with you and the venue to arrange reasonable accommodations such as accessible seating, dietary needs, or assistive listening devices.

policy_documents.title: Privacy Policy
policy_documents.content: We collect only the information needed to organize the reunion and provide personalized features such as the classmate directory and seating chart. Your contact details are visible only to site administrators and are never sold to third parties. You can control what classmates see on your profile using the visibility settings on the My Profile page.

policy_documents.title: Terms of Use
policy_documents.content: By using this reunion website, you agree to use it for lawful purposes related to the class reunion and associated events. You are responsible for the content you post and for keeping your account credentials secure. Organizers may modify or discontinue features at any time. Continued use of the site after updates to these terms constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

policy_documents.title: Community Code of Conduct
policy_documents.content: We are committed to creating a welcoming environment for all classmates. Harassment, hate speech, and discriminatory remarks are not tolerated on the website or at reunion events. Treat others with respect, even when you disagree. Moderators may remove content or restrict access for behavior that violates this code. Please report any concerns using the Contact & Support page.

profiles.bio: Back in Texas after a few moves around the country, working in product design and trying to balance work, friends, and travel.
Living in the Loop and working in nonprofit communications. Still obsessed with live music and weekend road trips.
Software engineer by day, rec league basketball devotee by night. Looking forward to catching up with everyone downtown.
Teaching middle school science in Evanston and spending weekends on the lakefront trail with friends and family.
Commuting into the city for work, raising two kids, and still sneaking in pickup soccer games when possible.
Pharmacist, occasional traveler, and enthusiastic home cook. Excited to reconnect with classmates around Chicago.

replies.body: I still remember getting completely lost on the way to the science wing and ending up in the band room instead. Someone was practicing the drum line cadence and it echoed through the whole hallway.
Pretty sure the best senior prank was when the entire class showed up in decade-themed outfits. The yearbook staff had no idea how to caption those photos.
Those marching band trips were legendary. The overnight bus ride to the state competition, card games in the aisle, and everyone trying to sleep sitting up are still some of my funniest memories.
I was in color guard, and I still hear the pep band tunes in my head whenever our old rival schools name pops up. I hope someone brings recordings to play at the reunion.
The yearbook blooper pages were my favorite. I remember one photo where half the class was mid-blink and they still used it because it was the only one where the banner wasnt falling down.
Did anyone else have their name spelled wrong under their senior photo? The yearbook staff felt so bad they gave me a free copy the next year.
Ill be there for the Friday reception and the late-night mixer but skipping the early campus tour. Looking forward to seeing who else makes it out right after work.
Same here  planning to arrive around 6:30 PM. If anyone wants to meet near the check-in desk first, Im in.
I booked at Harborview Inn & Suites since its walking distance and has free breakfast. If anyone else is staying there, we should coordinate walking over to the gala together.
Im at Millennium Park Lodge to keep the cost down. Its a little farther but still close enough and looks easy to get to the venue by rideshare.
I can help with check-in on Friday for an hour or two as long as it doesnt overlap too much with the welcome reception. Just let me know which shift you still need covered.
Happy to sign up for a Sunday morning shift for the service project. I like the idea of doing something for current students before everyone heads home.
Mr. Thompsons pop quizzes were stressful, but his stories about starting the jazz band and how it grew over the years were always inspiring.
I loved how Ms. Lee would sneak in our yearbook pages early so we could proofread them during homeroom. She probably saved a dozen embarrassing typos.

topics.title: First day of freshman year memories
topics.body: Walking into the building on our very first day felt like stepping into a maze. I still remember the smell of new textbooks, the too-big backpacks, and everyone pretending not to be nervous. What do you remember about that morning or your homeroom?

topics.title: Favorite senior prank ideas and stories
topics.body: Senior prank day was always risky but somehow our class pulled it off without getting detention. Share your favorite ideas that almost happened, the ones that actually did, or the pranks you heard about from other classes.

topics.title: Marching band trips and bus ride stories
topics.body: Marching band trips gave us some of the best stories: late-night bus rides, soggy uniforms in surprise rainstorms, and playing the fight song on repeat. Which trip stands out most in your memory, and does anyone still have photos or programs?

topics.title: Yearbook bloopers and awkward photos
topics.body: The yearbook staff caught so many accidental faces, awkward mid-sentence photos, and half-finished decorations. What are the funniest pictures, captions, or layout mistakes you remember seeing in any of our yearbooks?

topics.title: Who is coming to the Friday reception?
topics.body: Trying to get a head count for the Friday welcome reception and late-night mixer. Post here if you are planning to come on Friday, about what time you expect to arrive, and whether you are bringing a guest.

topics.title: Where are you staying for reunion weekend?
topics.body: If you have already booked a hotel or are staying with friends or family, share your plans here. It might help classmates coordinate carpools, split rideshares, or choose the same hotel to catch up in the lobby.

topics.title: Volunteering: we still need a little more help
topics.body: Organizers are looking for a few more volunteers to help with check-in, decorating the ballroom, and the Sunday service project. If you have an hour or two to spare, reply with your preferred day and time window.

topics.title: Favorite teacher stories and quotes
topics.body: Some of our teachers had legendary quotes, classroom rituals, or hilarious reactions when things went off the rails. Share your favorite stories about teachers who made a difference or just made you laugh when you needed it most.

topics.title: Funniest lunchroom stories and food memories
topics.body: The cafeteria line, mystery casseroles, and the occasional food fight gave us plenty to talk about. What are your funniest or strangest lunchroom memories, and who always managed to trade their way into the best desserts?

topics.title: Sunday brunch outfit ideas
topics.body: Totally lighthearted thread: is Sunday brunch more jeans-and-sneakers or dressy-casual? Share what you are thinking of wearing, especially if you are coming straight from checking out of your hotel.

topics.title: Carpools and trains from the suburbs
topics.body: For anyone driving in from the western or northern suburbs, use this topic to coordinate carpools or share train schedules. Mention your town, preferred arrival time, and whether you have extra seats.

topics.title: Introduce yourself and share a quick update
topics.body: If you have not posted here before, say hi and share a quick update about life since graduation. A sentence or two about where you are living, what you are up to, and what you are excited about for the reunion is perfect.

topics.title: Draft reunion schedule is now posted
topics.body: The draft schedule for the reunion weekend is now live on the Schedule & Events page. Times may shift slightly as we confirm details with the venue, but you can start building your personal agenda and planning travel.

volunteer_shifts.description: Welcome classmates, hand out name badges, and answer basic schedule questions during the first hour of the reception.
Assist with late arrivals checking in after work and help direct guests to the welcome reception.
Support early badge pickup and welcome out-of-town classmates arriving on Thursday.
Lead small groups around the high school campus and share basic information about new spaces and old favorites.
Help set up tables, lawn games, and signage in the park before families arrive for the picnic.
Staff the information table, direct people to events, and answer questions about tickets and the schedule.

volunteer_shifts.role_name: Gala Room Setup & Decor – Saturday Afternoon
volunteer_shifts.description: Assist with decorating the ballroom, placing centerpieces, and setting out programs before the gala.

volunteer_shifts.role_name: Final Gala Prep – Saturday Late Afternoon
volunteer_shifts.description: Finish last-minute decorations, check reserved tables, and coordinate with catering staff prior to guest arrival.
Help direct cars to appropriate lots and greet classmates as they arrive for afternoon activities.
Set up tables and supplies for assembling school supply kits before the main group arrives.
Join the main service project shift to assemble school supply kits for current students.
Help clear tables, pack up decorations, and load remaining supplies after the brunch ends.
